questionTextSource: What will be the effect if the brake line hose with the yellow coupling head tears between the towing vehicle and trailer during braking?

answerCSource: Every 24 months

questionTextSource: What must be clearly marked in buses?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: The location of the first-aid kits
answerBSource: Seats for severely disabled persons in regular traffic
answerCSource: Location of the on-board tools

questionTextSource: What must you check at the emergency exits before setting off?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Whether they have the devices necessary for opening
answerBSource: Whether they are covered by luggage or other objects
answerCSource: Whether they are lit as required

questionTextSource: A bus has 15 passenger seats. What is the minimum number of standard first-aid kits required?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: 1 first-aid kit
answerBSource: No first-aid kit is required up to a number of 16 passenger seats

questionTextSource: What is the minimum number of operative fire extinguishers which must be carried on a bus (single deck)?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: A total of 1 fire extinguisher
answerBSource: From 12 passenger seats onwards, 2 fire extinguishers

questionTextSource: What is the location of the required fire extinguishers on a double-decker bus?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Near the driver’s seat
answerBSource: Another one on the upper passenger deck
answerCSource: Another one downstairs before the rear staircase

questionTextSource: Where can you find the details of the inspection period of a fire extinguisher carried in the vehicle?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: On the inspection tag of the fire extinguisher
answerBSource: On the maintenance log of the fire extinguisher
answerCSource: On the adhesive seal in the registration certificate Part I

questionTextSource: You are driving a double-decker bus. Where should a fire extinguisher be located?
explanationSource: According to Section 35g of the Road Traffic Licensing Regulations (StVZO), double-decker buses must carry at least two operational fire extinguishers with a filling mass of 6 kg each. A fire extinguisher should be placed in the immediate vicinity of the driver's seat to provide the driver with quick access in an emergency. The second fire extinguisher must be located on the upper passenger level so that rapid action can be taken there in the event of a fire. This arrangement ensures that both the driver and passengers on both levels of the bus can act quickly in the event of a fire to increase the safety of all occupants.
answerASource: In the immediate vicinity of the driver’s seat
answerBSource: On the upper deck
answerCSource: In the luggage compartment

questionTextSource: What is the normal trial period?
explanationSource: Normally, the probationary period is 2 years. But there are also situations where it can be extended to up to 4 years.
answerASource: 2 years
answerBSource: 1 year
answerCSource: 3 years

questionTextSource: You are currently in the probationary period and to date have not come to the attention of the authorities. What consequences may result if you fail to observe the traffic sign "Stop. Give way." and thereby endanger other road users?
explanationSource: Failure to observe the traffic sign "Stop. Grant right of way" is a serious violation, an A violation, which is recorded in the driving qualifying register. In the event of an A-violation or two minor violations (B-violations) within the probationary period, you will have to attend a retraining seminar as a novice driver. You will not have to take an exam again.
answerASource: An order to attend an advanced seminar for new drivers
answerBSource: Entry into the Central Register of Road Traffic Offenders
answerCSource: An order to resit a theoretical driving licence test

questionTextSource: What should be achieved by attending an advanced seminar during the probationary period?
explanationSource: At the retraining seminar you will learn how to analyze your daily driving behavior and raise your awareness of dangerous situations in the probationary period. Points cannot be deducted with the seminar. This can be done in the driving ability seminar.
answerASource: A more considerate and risk-conscious attitude to driving on public roads
answerBSource: Improved risk awareness
answerCSource: Reducing the number of points in the Central Register of Road Traffic Offenders

questionTextSource: Having committed several violations during the probationary period, the authorities have ordered a driver to attend an advanced seminar. The new driver fails to comply with the order. What may he now anticipate?
explanationSource: If the novice driver fails to attend the retraining seminar as required, he is threatened with the immediate loss of his driving license. An additional fine will not be imposed. Also, no points will be entered in the register of driver fitness.
answerASource: The withdrawal of the driving licence
answerBSource: A notice to pay a public fine
answerCSource: Additional points in the Central Register of Road Traffic Offenders

questionTextSource: What should a driving licence applicant anticipate if he is found driving without a licence?
explanationSource: A person who wants to obtain a driving license not only has to bring along the necessary physical and mental requirements. In addition, he must not have repeatedly violated traffic regulations or penal laws. This would show that the applicant is not suitable for driving a motor vehicle. The same applies to driving without a license. Should a person, who wants to obtain a driving license, drive without one, the authorities might not allow you to attend the exam.
answerASource: The driving licence authority may withdraw the application inspection order
answerBSource: A test may be ordered to ascertain your aptitude for driving motor vehicles
answerCSource: No consequences, as long as you are registered with a driving school

questionTextSource: A learner driver has committed several violations during the probationary period. The authority has ordered that the driver attends a supplementary driving course. The learner driver fails to comply with this order. What can they expect to happen?
explanationSource: The probationary period for novice drivers is 2 years. During this time, very strict penalties from the catalogue of fines apply. If you commit an A offence or two B offences during the probationary period, you will be ordered to attend an advanced training seminar (ASF). If you do not comply with this official order, your driving licence will be revoked.
answerASource: The withdrawal of their driving licence
answerBSource: An extension of the probationary period by another 3 years
answerCSource: An order to attend a driver fitness session

questionTextSource: A driving licence applicant practices practical driving skills without a driving instructor on public roads. What should he expect if the police perform a check on him?
explanationSource:
As a driving licence applicant, you are not allowed to drive on public roads without a instructor. Driving without a license is a criminal offence and can be punished with a fine or prison sentence.
The police can order a check of your ability to drive. As a driving license applicant, you run the risk of never receiving a driving license.
answerASource: A criminal prosecution
answerBSource: A review of his fitness to drive a motor vehicle
answerCSource: No consequences provided he is accompanied in the vehicle by a holder of a driving licence

questionTextSource: You are transporting commercial goods with a truck. What is the purpose of the 35-hour further training course, which you must attend within 5 years?
explanationSource: When transporting commercial goods, the aim of the 35-hour training course is to increase health awareness and refresh knowledge of the regulations for freight transport.
answerASource: To increase awareness of health
answerBSource: To refresh my knowledge of the road haulage regulations
answerCSource: To acquire additional driving licence classes

questionTextSource: Working as a professional driver requires you to complete a basic qualification and to regularly attend training programmes. Where will you find information concerning the validity period of your professional driver’s basic qualification?
explanationSource: You can find the information about the time limit on the back of your driving licence.
answerASource: On my driving licence
answerBSource: On the driver card
answerCSource: On the website of the Federal Motor Transport Authority

questionTextSource: You are a commercial bus driver. What is the purpose of the 35-hour further training course, which you must attend within 5 years?
explanationSource: If you are transporting people commercially by bus, within 35-hour training course you will learn how to increase health awareness and refresh your knowledge of the regulations for passenger transport. Participation is compulsory.
answerASource: To increase awareness of health
answerBSource: To refresh my knowledge of the passenger transport regulations
answerCSource: To acquire additional driving licence classes

questionTextSource: What can lead to a review of your fitness to be a bus driver?
answerASource: - my physical capabilities
answerBSource: - my mental capacity
answerCSource: - the reliability of my employer

questionTextSource: When can the authorities order a bus driver to undergo a fitness assessment?
answerASource: If the driver has seriously violated road traffic regulations
answerBSource: If the driver has committed a serious road traffic offence
answerCSource: If there are indications that the bus driver has a high potential for aggression

questionTextSource: After receiving your Class C driving licence for the first time, you want to transport goods commercially. How can you meet the requirements?
answerASource: - the accelerated initial qualification
answerBSource: - a professional driver training programme
answerCSource: - a 35-hour advanced training course

questionTextSource: After receiving your Class D driving licence for the first time, you want to transport passengers commercially. How can you meet the requirements?
answerASource: - the accelerated initial qualification
answerBSource: - a professional driver training programme
answerCSource: - a 35-hour advanced training course

questionTextSource: To be employed as a professional driver, you will need an initial qualification as well as regular training. How are the relevant proofs documented?
explanationSource: Professional drivers who work in the transport of goods or passengers on public roads require an initial qualification and then regular further training. The validity of these trainings are documented on the driver qualification certificate.
answerASource: On the driver qualification card
answerBSource: In the Federal Motor Vehicle and Transport Authority’s professional driver qualification register (KBA)
answerCSource: On the webpage of my driver licensing agency
